_comment: World geography for Grizzly Knights — a modernized, street-level Marvel NYC. Maps the roster to turf so the sim spawns characters in-place and the 3D world knows what to build. Stems reference universe/characters/. Districts are spawn regions; factions are the institutional layer.

districts.name: Hell's Kitchen
districts.vibe: Street-level crucible — Catholic guilt, working-class grit, the Hand's shadow, gentrification's edge. The beating heart of the ground-level world.

districts.name: Harlem
districts.vibe: Community, resilience, the cost of being a hero where you're from. Protection, not domination.

districts.name: Chinatown / Lower Manhattan
districts.vibe: K'un-Lun's bleed-through into the modern city — martial mysticism under neon, old debts.

districts.name: Brooklyn
districts.vibe: The next generation and the old guard's roots — where the future Spider and the first Captain both come from.

districts.name: Queens (Forest Hills)
districts.vibe: Outer-borough ordinariness over extraordinary weight — the most human corner of the map.

districts.name: Midtown / Manhattan core
districts.vibe: Power, money, spectacle — towers and headlines. Where institutions live.

districts.name: Greenwich Village
districts.vibe: The thin place — where the rational city meets the occult.

districts.name: Westchester (greater NYC)
districts.vibe: The dream of coexistence behind a wrought-iron gate — and the fault line under it.

districts.name: The shadow city (mobile / supernatural)
districts.vibe: Not a neighborhood — the night layer that crosses all of them. Hunters and the hunted.

factions.name: The Brotherhood
factions.note: Magneto's answer to a world that made him — not wrong, and he knows it at his core.

factions.name: The Daily Bugle
factions.note: Jameson, Robbie Robertson, Betty Brant generate into this faction as supporting cast lands.

factions.name: Cosmic (Earthfall)
factions.note: Not based on Earth — but any of them can show up, and have.
